beats.activity: Wake up, shower, strong coffee—prepping for a hot day ahead.
beats.reasoning: Morning routine in Greenwood; energy was moderate yesterday (50/100), so starting with deliberate self-care and caffeine. Clear, hot weather ahead means hydration and planning matter.

beats.activity: Deep work on grant proposals from home office, breaking for water and stretching.
beats.reasoning: Nathan works from home; the heat and air quality make staying indoors productive. Four hours of focused grant writing fits his hyper-focused personality and avoids midday heat exposure.

beats.activity: Quick lunch at home—salad and cold drink to beat the heat.
beats.reasoning: Staying cool indoors during peak heat hours (91F by afternoon). Light meal and hydration are practical responses to the weather forecast.

beats.activity: Commute to Renton office for afternoon client meeting.
beats.reasoning: Regular workplace commute; 35 min budgeted for Greenwood to Renton drive. Midday timing avoids worst heat while allowing afternoon work.

beats.activity: Client meeting and grant strategy session at nonprofit office.
beats.reasoning: Scheduled in-person work; Nathan's role involves direct collaboration with nonprofit staff on grant proposals. Two hours allows substantive feedback and planning.
Return drive from Renton to Greenwood; 35 min budgeted. Afternoon traffic should be light at this hour.

beats.activity: Quick errand: pick up cold drinks and snacks at QFC for evening.
beats.reasoning: Practical stop for hydration and food before heading out. Neighborhood errand on the way home; fits the heat-response planning.

beats.activity: Rest and freshen up at home; update expense spreadsheet and tidy.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down time before evening out; Nathan values organization and would track today's expenses. Resting in AC before heading to outdoor concert when it cools slightly.

beats.activity: Commute to Gas Works Park for outdoor concert.
beats.reasoning: 30 min travel from Greenwood to Wallingford; timing arrives just as concert starts at 6pm. Clear skies and cooling evening temps make outdoor leisure appealing.

beats.activity: Enjoy free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park; socialize, people-watch, stay hydrated.
beats.reasoning: Nathan values close friends over big crowds, but a free concert on a clear evening fits his interests. 2.25 hours allows genuine leisure without overcommitting to the crowded scene he'd naturally avoid.
Evening return drive; 45 min budgeted for post-event traffic leaving the park. Gets Nathan home before 21:30.

world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
